question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released on April 10, 2026, showing that <cite index="4-1,4-6,14-1,14-6">the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. Since 0.3% is significantly less than the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. <cite index="3-3">The Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland's Inflation Nowcasting tool now projects the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate will jump from 2.4% in February to 3.16% in March</cite>, but the monthly increase was only 0.3%, well below the 0.8% needed.
key_factors: Official March 2026 CPI data already released showing 0.3% monthly increase
Market resolution criteria requires more than 0.8% monthly increase
0.3% actual result is far below 0.8% threshold
Market should resolve to NO with certainty
risks: Potential data revisions (though unlikely to change by 0.5+ percentage points)
Market may take time to adjust to reflect the known outcome
Possible confusion about whether data has been officially released
